full_text:
Heatwave intensifies in Gujarat as temperatures cross 39°C in eight cities
Summer temperatures in Gujarat have begun to rise, surpassing 39°C in eight cities on Monday, as per the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Rajkot recorded the highest temperature in the state at 40°C, making it the hottest city of the day. In Ahmedabad, the maximum temperature touched 39.4°C, which was 5.2°C above the normal level.
IMD has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in several parts of the state. Districts likely to experience heatwave conditions include Rajkot, Kutch, Gandhinagar, Banaskantha, Vadodara, Surat, Amreli, Surendranagar, Bhavnagar and Botad.
According to the forecast, temperatures in Ahmedabad are expected to rise further and may reach around 41°C over the next two days.
The department has also issued a yellow alert for the state for the next three days as temperatures are likely to exceed 40°C in many areas.
Authorities have advised residents to take precautions against the heatwave, including avoiding unnecessary outdoor activities between 12 noon and 4 pm, wearing light cotton clothing and consuming adequate fluids such as water, buttermilk and lemonade to stay hydrated.

title: Heatwave alert for A’bad, G’nagar as mercury hits 39.4°C - Ahmedabad Mirror

full_text:
Ahmedabad Others
Heatwave alert for A’bad, G’nagar as mercury hits 39.4°C
IMD warns of rising temperatures over next three days; hot and humid weather likely across Gujarat
Hottest places in Gujarat on Sunday; Pic: Nilkanth Dave
Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar are likely to face heatwave conditions over the next two days as temperatures in the city have already climbed close to the 40°C mark. According to the latest weather bulletin, Ahmedabad recorded a maximum temperature of 39.4°C on Sunday, which is 5.2°C above normal, signalling the early arrival of intense summer heat in the region. The minimum temperature was recorded at 19.1°C, slightly above normal.
The weather department has issued a heatwave warning for isolated pockets of Ahmedabad, Gandhinagar, Banaskantha, Vadodara and Surat on Monday and Tuesday. Similar conditions are also expected in parts of Saurashtra-Kutch, including Rajkot, Amreli, Surendranagar, Bhavnagar, Botad and Kutch.
The forecast suggests that maximum and minimum temperatures across Gujarat may rise further by 2–3°C over the next three days, after which no major change is expected. Dry weather is likely to continue across all districts of Gujarat, including Saurashtra-Kutch and the Union Territories of Diu, Daman and Dadra & Nagar Haveli for the coming week.
Weather officials have also indicated that hot and humid conditions may develop in coastal areas of Gujarat and Saurashtra-Kutch for the upcoming three days. With temperatures already nearing the 40°C mark, residents of Ahmedabad and nearby areas may experience hotter days ahead as summer tightens its grip over the state.

title: Season’s first heatwave is here; Yellow Alert issued for Ahmedabad - The Times of India

full_text:
Ahmedabad: If you have been feeling that the past few days have been a bit too hot for early March, the sun plans to crank up its heat even more in what the weather office is calling the season's first yellow alert. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast ‘sporadic heatwave impact' in Ahmedabad and several other districts for Monday and Tuesday. A yellow alert was issued for Kutch and Rajkot on Sunday.
This alert for Monday and Tuesday covers Kutch, Banaskantha, Gandhinagar, Ahmedabad, Surendranagar, Rajkot, Amerli, Bhavnagar, Botad, Vadodara, and Surat. These districts may experience temperatures above normal, with hot and humid conditions forecast for coastal areas.
Temperatures are likely to stabilize from March 11 onwards, according to IMD.
On Sunday, Ahmedabad recorded a maximum temperature of 39.4 degrees celsius, which was 5.2 degrees above normal. At 19.1 degrees celsius, the minimum temperature was 1.2 degrees higher than normal. The forecast indicated a gradual rise in maximum and minimum temperatures by two to three degrees during the next three days. Thereafter, there will be no large change.
In Rajkot and Surendranagar, the mercury touched 40 degrees celsius on Sunday for the first time this summer. Following closely was Mahua (39.8 degrees) and Deesa (39.6). IMD's long-range forecast for March indicates chances of above-normal temperatures for the majority of regions of Gujarat state.

full_text:
Heatwave Warning: IMD Issues Yellow Alert for Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara and Parts of Gujarat
March 09, 2026
Ahmedabad: If the last few days have felt unusually hot for early March, the heat is set to intensify further as the weather office has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in several cities of Gujarat. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast a sporadic heatwave impact in Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara and several other districts on Monday and Tuesday, along with parts of Saurashtra.
In its bulletin, the IMD stated, “Heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets over the Gujarat region, namely Ahmedabad, Gandhinagar, Banaskantha, Vadodara and Surat, and over Saurashtra-Kutch districts including Amreli, Rajkot, Surendranagar, Bhavnagar, Botad and Kutch” on March 9 and 10.”
Furthermore, the IMD said that hot and humid conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated places in the coastal areas of Saurashtra-Kutch from March 9 to March 11.
On Sunday, Ahmedabad recorded a maximum temperature of 39.4°C, which was 5.2 degrees above normal, while the minimum temperature last night stood at 21.8°C, about 3.9 degrees above normal. The forecast indicates a gradual rise of 2–3 degrees in both maximum and minimum temperatures over the next three days, after which no major change is expected.
In Rajkot and Surendranagar, the mercury touched 40°C for the first time this season on Sunday. Mahuva recorded 39.8°C, while Deesa reported 39.6°C. Surat, Vadodara and state capital Gandhinagar also recorded maximum temperatures above 39°C.
The IMD’s long-range outlook for March suggests a high probability of above-normal temperatures across most parts of Gujarat, indicating an early onset of intense summer conditions. DeshGujarat
